seoTitle: Frazers Creek vs Freeburn Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Frazers Creek and Freeburn Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Frazers Creek vs Freeburn Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frazers Creek and Freeburn Island.
heading: Frazers Creek vs Freeburn Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frazers Creek, New South Wales and Freeburn Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freeburn Island has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Frazers Creek (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frazers Creek or Freeburn Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frazers Creek has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Freeburn Island scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frazers Creek scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Freeburn Island scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frazers Creek or Freeburn Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freeburn Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
